title: Mechanism of artificial surface defect induced cracking for very high cycle fatigue of Ti alloys
excerpt: In-situ observation and EBSD analysis reveal that VHCF failure from artificial surface defects is driven by cyclic stress–environment synergy, with crack growth consuming less than 20,000 cycles.
citation: Sun, J., Peng, W., & Sun, C. (2022). Mechanism of artificial surface defect induced cracking for very high cycle fatigue of Ti alloys. Engineering Fracture Mechanics, 272, 108721.

title: Effects of Notches and Defects on Dwell Fatigue Mechanism and Fatigue Life of Ti-6Al-4V ELI Alloy Used in Deep-Sea Submersibles
venue: Journal of Marine Science and Engineering
excerpt: Dwell fatigue is shown to be insensitive to defect size (190–438 μm), and notched specimens exhibit higher dwell fatigue life than smooth specimens at the same local stress—critical for pressure hull design.
citation: Sun, J., Wu, L., & Sun, C. (2021). Effects of Notches and Defects on Dwell Fatigue Mechanism and Fatigue Life of Ti-6Al-4V ELI Alloy Used in Deep-Sea Submersibles. Journal of Marine Science and Engineering, 9(8), 845.

title: A method of quasi in-situ EBSD observation for microstructure and damage evolution in fatigue and dwell fatigue of Ti alloys
excerpt: A novel quasi in-situ EBSD method captures the evolution of twinning and slip, revealing that twinning occurs in α-grains with prismatic Schmid factor ≤ 0.2 and c-axis angle ≤ 46°.
citation: Sun, C., Sun, J., Chi, W., Wang, J., & Wang, W. (2023). A method of quasi in-situ EBSD observation for microstructure and damage evolution in fatigue and dwell fatigue of Ti alloys. International Journal of Fatigue, 176, 107897.

title: Effects of Natural Aging and Discontinuous Cyclic Loading on High Cycle Fatigue Behavior of Steels
excerpt: Long-term natural aging (>20,000 h) reduces HCF life in 25CrMo4 and 30CrMnSiA steels via surface corrosion, while discontinuous loading has no harmful effect on fatigue life.
citation: Li, G., Liu, J., Sun, J., & Sun, C. (2023). Effects of Natural Aging and Discontinuous Cyclic Loading on High Cycle Fatigue Behavior of Steels. Metals, 13(3), 511.
